definition: For samples represented by multiple vectors, retrieve candidate train samples using fixed-dimensional encodings, vector-set sketches, compressed late-interaction indexes, or multi-vector graph search, then compute the intended MaxSim or set-of-vectors score on candidates.

scale_profile.best_for: Audits where each scientific unit is a bag, sequence, region, document, image, protein, or cell represented by many local embeddings.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large corpora with millions of objects when local vectors are compressed, pooled, or indexed with candidate generation.
scale_profile.avoid_when: A single pooled embedding is already the chosen scientific similarity definition.

tunable_parameters.name: local_vector_budget
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls token, patch, domain, or instance vectors retained per sample.

tunable_parameters.name: candidate_count
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls how many train samples are reranked with the exact late-interaction scorer.

tunable_parameters.name: compression_or_encoding
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects pooling, fixed-dimensional encoding, residual compression, bit vectors, or graph search.

quality_gates: Compare candidate recall and reranked scores against brute-force multi-vector scoring on a subset.
Report whether local vectors were pooled, token-pruned, quantized, or compressed.
Check that the chosen compression does not erase the local feature driving the scientific novelty axis.
failure_modes: Pooling can hide local novelty even if global similarity remains high.
Candidate retrieval can miss train samples that only match through rare local vectors.
Late-interaction scores may not be symmetric, so the direction of train-eval comparison must be declared.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py only for a pooled-vector baseline; production multi-vector search should export candidate-reranked MaxSim or set-of-vectors scores in the same SPECTRA format.

citations.title: MUVERA: Multi-Vector Retrieval via Fixed Dimensional Encodings
citations.evidence: Approximates multi-vector retrieval using fixed-dimensional encodings, reducing candidate retrieval latency while preserving recall.

citations.title: DESSERT: An Efficient Algorithm for Vector Set Search with Vector Set Queries
citations.evidence: Introduces efficient search for sets of vectors with vector-set queries.

citations.title: WARP: An Efficient Engine for Multi-Vector Retrieval
citations.evidence: Uses compressed late-interaction retrieval and two-stage reduction to accelerate multi-vector scoring.
